Incident Report (Operational/Cyber) — Ashbury Markets plc (UK Main Market issuer) — Internal Only; UK MAR assessment included.
On 06/06/2026 09:52 BST, anomalous access to “Project Moorland” M&A folder (unannounced disposal of Ridgefell Underwriting Ltd; expected £48–52m impairment) was logged from a non-standard network route with potential price sensitivity.
Mr. Rowan Hexton (DOB 14/03/1989; Deputy Head of Treasury; Ashbury Markets plc; based at 5 Ledger Street, Eastborough EX0 1ZZ) reports his company laptop asset ASB-LDN-LAP-1171 (Lanturn Pro 14) showing mail UPN rowan.hexton@ashburymarkets.example active in VDI session vdi-ldn14-20260606-0951.
The event correlated with MFA push fatigue on handset Quoratek X12‑SE IMEI 3520 9900 1234 567 (truncated); no MAC addresses were captured, and this does not constitute a material adverse change to group outlook.
Security log ref SOC-INC-74291 shows DocShare URL https://intra.ashburymarkets.example/ds/proj/moorland?v=9&uid=r.hexton&id=FOL-334A and TeamSpace meeting link https://meet.ashburymarkets.example/7f3b?sid=vd i - l d n 1 4 - 0 6 0 6 - 0 9 5 1 (OCR-like spacing preserved).
At 10:14 BST we updated the Article 18 insider list (Section A: deal team), paused wall-crossing, and saved an RNS draft; Article 17 disclosure is withheld pending containment per the Data Protection Act and UK financial regulator guidance.
The suspected exfiltration volume was 23.4MB from file “Ridgefell-Impairment-Model-v11.xlsx”; hash 1e:2b:7c:fa:…; DLP policy FIN-UK-GDPR-PII-05 triggered on columns containing salary bands and employee initials.
Contact: secops@ashburymarkets.example; out-of-hours +44 7000 900 111 (routing to Eastborough SOC); ticket mirror at http://servicedesk.ashburymarkets.example/ticket?i d = I N C % 2 D 7 4 2 9 1 & user = r h e x t o n (spaces intentional).
Ms. Tamsin Graye (Group Compliance Officer) confirmed a preliminary STOR is not required at this stage, requested SMCR attestations from Mr. Hexton and the deal lead, and initiated an ICO liaison under breach-threshold review.
We note a benign training dataset on a decommissioned share (DOB 11/11/1992; GLA-TAB-2003; location: North Docks; account ops‑temp‑04) that is not linked to any named person and should not be treated as personal data.
Generic demographics cited in a marketing slide (age 29, lives in the south east, uses Android) are not identifying on their own and are irrelevant to this incident.
project notes (lowercase): defer sprint 14 backlog until post‑closure; move Falcon Crest dashboard to read‑only; product names: LedgerLite, PortFlow—these terms are public and non‑sensitive.
No customer accounts or payment card data were accessed; we are not aware of any misuse, and endpoint scans show no persistence; importantly, the word “inside” in a filename (inside_out_jacket.jpg) is not related to inside information.
Next steps: rotate credentials for ASB‑LDN‑LAP‑1171; reset Mr. Hexton’s MFA seed; refresh information barriers around Project Moorland; legal to reassess UK MAR disclosure at 16:00 BST and prepare a contingency RNS.
